Patch Set 1:

While the patch looks like it is not going to break anything, it isn't clear to my why multiop would behave differently than cat?  Would this not also cause requests to be kept in the RPC cache?  Why is multiop going to avoid the cache?

Also, a patch to fix a test that is failing intermittently should run the test multiple times to see if the patch is actually fixing the problem, since it may just be randomly passing and not actually fixed. 

 Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity

Unfortunately, this test is failing so rarely (only 4 times in the past 4 weeks, 0.00% of last 100 runs) that even this is unlikely to trigger a failure.

Patch Set 1:

Sorry for delayed response.
the meaning of using "multiop" instead of "cat" is to reduce the bl callback of locks.
I have just looked at the logs again and found most of the locks are the LOOKUP|LAYOUT lock
gotten when opening the file, then using the "multiop" won't help, will update a new patch soon.
Uploaded patch set 2.

commit.subject: LU-6941 test: don't return layout lock in open
commit.message:
LU-6941 test: don't return layout lock in open

In test_209 of sanity, the lock bl callback requests triggered
by the lock acquired when doing IO in "cat" call, the blocking
callback will cause some requests to be cached in history and
affect the count calculation of requests.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity,sanity

Change-Id: I7fa3d1dabb8e295ead43bc24731b443d83641563
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao.zhang@intel.com>
